creator: filis@FreeBSD.org
text:
it's odd. I also just now tried to reproduce it in a new 14.0 and 13.3 jail with mysql80-server-8.0.39 and bind-tools-9.18.28 (both installed from -latest packages) and mysql starts fine with or without bind-tools (which brings protobuf-24.4,1 and protobuf-c-1.4.1_2 as dependencies).
When we ran into this issue, we had bind-tools-9.18.28, protobuf-c-1.4.1_2 and protobuf-24.4,1 installed. When we deinstalled them, the problem went away.
